patch.value: Found in audit #15 follow-up: pm update --body-file - fails with ENOENT (tries to open a file literally named '-'), while pm notes --add - reads stdin. Agents composing bodies from heredocs/pipes (the grep/jq/heredoc composability principle) hit this asymmetry and fall back to temp files. Inventory every --*-file flag via pm contracts, route them through a shared read-file-or-stdin helper, and record the convention in the scripting contract. Related-not-duplicate: pm-kipd (GH-438 full-item JSON on stdin), pm-a2h3 (notes/learnings --stdin parity).
Uniform stdin convention: every --*-file flag (body-file, close --reason-file, etc.) accepts '-' for stdin, matching the annotation --add - convention

All file-reading flags across commands accept '-' to read stdin (inventory taken from contracts); behavior documented in the scripting contract (pm-psy1); error message for unreadable paths mentions the '-' option; tests cover piped heredoc usage; no behavior change for real paths

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Standardize stdin handling so every file-valued flag that accepts external content can use '-' for stdin, giving scripts and agents one predictable convention for generated bodies, reasons, and documents.

message: Backfill description during all-status ecosystem audit

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Dogfood confirmation 2026-07-15 (review pass #89): pm create --body-file - fails ENOENT body_file_unreadable (tries to open a file literally named "-"), forcing a scratch-file workaround mid-review. The error's own next_steps suggest "ls -l \"-\"" — the recovery bundle does not know stdin is the intent. This is exactly the convention gap this item owns; --body-file should be first on the flag list when implementing.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: uniform --*-file stdin convention and multi-value filter grammar are the same CLI input-grammar-consistency class
